assertions.expectation: Checks for deprecated API usage that may have changed behavior in 1.30
assertions.reason: Response includes specific commands to check for deprecated API usage: 'kubectl get --raw /metrics | grep apiserver_requested_deprecated_apis' and mentions checking audit logs for deprecated API usage

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking admission webhooks — they may be rejecting or slowing down requests on the new version
assertions.reason: Response has a dedicated section on admission webhook failures with specific commands to check webhook configurations and recent webhook failures

assertions.expectation: Advises checking if resource requests/limits need adjusting for any system components that changed
assertions.reason: Response mentions checking resource utilization with 'kubectl top nodes' and 'kubectl top pods' but doesn't specifically address adjusting resource requests/limits for system components

assertions.expectation: Suggests examining kube-system pods for any that are crashlooping or restarting
assertions.reason: Response includes commands to check kube-system pods: 'kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l component=kube-apiserver' and 'kubectl get pods -n kube-system | grep -E "gke-|cilium|calico|flannel"'

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to check API server metrics and latency (kubectl get --raw /metrics)
assertions.reason: Response includes specific commands: 'kubectl get --raw /metrics | grep -E "apiserver_request_duration|apiserver_request_total|etcd_request_duration"' and other metrics commands

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking if NetworkPolicy or service mesh components need updating for the new version
assertions.reason: Response has a dedicated section on service mesh/CNI issues with commands to check network plugin pods and service mesh components like Istio

assertions.expectation: Advises reviewing pod events for scheduling delays or readiness probe failures
assertions.reason: Response includes commands to check pod events: 'kubectl get events -A --sort-by='.lastTimestamp'' and 'kubectl get events -A --field-selector reason=Unhealthy'

assertions.expectation: Mentions checking if HPA or VPA are behaving differently on the new version
assertions.reason: Response includes specific commands to check HPA behavior: 'kubectl get hpa -A' and 'kubectl describe hpa HPA_NAME -n NAMESPACE'
